---
id: BUG-008
title: active packet with no lease is a CLI deadlock: takeover must adopt abandoned active work
depends_on: []

---

## Task
Found by dogfooding 2026-07-10 (orchestrator relaunch): `task release` on an ACTIVE packet leaves it active WITHOUT a lease, and that state is a CLI deadlock — takeover refuses ("no lease to take over"), start refuses (not ready), and every transition out of active refuses ("lease required to leave active"). The only exits would be raw SQL (forbidden, closed world). A successor session can never adopt abandoned active work.
Fix the class:
1. `takeoverPacket`: when the packet is ACTIVE and no lease exists, ACQUIRE the lease for the calling session (evented as takeover `from none`); keep all current refusals for live leases.
2. `task release` on an active packet warns in its output that the packet stays active and names takeover as the adoption path (no behavior change — release remains valid for handoff).

## RED test (write first)
In `src/tasks/service.takeover.test.ts` add a test named exactly: "takeover adopts an active packet whose lease was released". Start a packet, release the lease, assert active with no lease, then takeover from a second session: assert the lease now belongs to it and a takeover event exists. Today takeover throws 'no lease to take over' -> it FAILS.
In `src/cli/commands/task.test.ts`, extend the release test so it asserts the release output names the `task takeover <ID>` adoption path.
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the test name "takeover adopts an active packet whose lease was released".

## Reuse
takeoverPacket / releaseLease in src/tasks/service.ts; task release handler in src/cli/commands/task.ts; the events table; existing lease test helpers.

## Stop conditions
Weakening the live-lease protections (force still required for non-stale live leases); a second lease-acquisition path; touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
